trade.summary: Strong bilateral trade with the US as Sweden's largest single-country export destination outside the EU. Sweden exports pharmaceuticals, machinery, vehicles, and defence equipment to the US; the US exports aircraft, machinery, chemicals, and software to Sweden. Swedish multinationals (Ericsson, Volvo, AstraZeneca) have major US operations.

diplomatic.summary: Sweden joined NATO in March 2024, deepening an already close partnership. The US was a strong advocate for Sweden's NATO accession. Bilateral relations are anchored in shared democratic values, liberal trade, and common security interests. Sweden aligns closely with US positions on Russia, China technology policy, and global governance. The relationship gained new strategic depth post-2022.

military.summary: Full NATO allies since March 2024. The US and Sweden have significantly expanded defence cooperation since Russia's invasion of Ukraine. US forces have access to Swedish bases. Sweden is acquiring advanced US systems including the Patriot air defence system and cooperates closely on Baltic Sea security. Joint exercises are extensive under NATO frameworks.

financial.summary: Deep financial ties. Swedish firms have massive US operations and US companies are major investors in Sweden. AP pension funds and the Riksbank hold large USD-denominated assets. Silicon Valley tech firms have major Sweden presences. Bilateral investment is among the largest in the transatlantic relationship per capita.

energy.summary: Growing energy cooperation. Sweden imports some US LNG as part of European gas diversification post-Ukraine war. US firms invest in Sweden's offshore wind sector. Cooperation on nuclear energy technology through Westinghouse and Sweden's nuclear programme.

scientific.summary: Among the world's deepest bilateral research relationships per capita. Joint work at leading institutions in biomedical sciences, physics, climate research, and digital technology. Karolinska, KTH, and Uppsala have extensive US university partnerships. Many Nobel laureates have US-Sweden research connections.
